name: International Bank Of Commerce
description_short: FDIC-insured regional bank serving Oklahoma and Texas with personal, business, and international banking services including checking, savings, mortgages, and investment products.
description_long: International Bank of Commerce (IBC Bank) is a regional financial institution headquartered in Laredo, Texas, with operations across Oklahoma and Texas. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. IBC Bank operates as a subsidiary of International Bancshares Corporation (NASDAQ: IBOC), a publicly traded company that regularly announces shareholder dividends. IBC Bank offers comprehensive personal banking services including multiple checking account options (Free Checking, Rite Checking, Regular Checking, Interest Checking), savings accounts, certificates of deposit, IRAs, and currency exchange services. On the business side, they provide commercial checking, business savings, small business loans, merchant services, treasury management tools (including remote deposit and lockbox services), and business investment products (401ks, profit sharing plans, simple IRAs). Their international banking division specifically serves customers with cross-border needs, offering specialized checking accounts, certificates of deposit, letters of credit, wire transfers, and a foreign national loan program. Personal and business credit card products are available through their Visa partnership. What distinguishes IBC Bank is its dual focus on serving both the U.S. domestic market and international customers, particularly those with cross-border financial needs. The bank offers specialized services like currency exchange and foreign cash limit certifications not commonly found at mainstream banks. Their product suite spans personal finance through business investments and insurance products (garage insurance, workers compensation, property insurance, business auto insurance, Medicare supplement insurance), positioning them as a one-stop financial services provider for their regional markets. As a regional bank, IBC Bank serves Oklahoma and Texas markets with established brick-and-mortar locations and digital banking options (online and mobile banking). The company demonstrates stability through consistent dividend announcements to shareholders. However, as a regional institution, they lack the national branch network of larger banks and may have more limited product sophistication compared to national competitors. Their website emphasizes financial education through Visa's Practical Money Skills program, suggesting a customer education-focused approach.
services: FDIC-insured checking accounts (Free, Rite, Regular, and Interest Checking)
Savings accounts and certificates of deposit (personal and business)
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and retirement planning products
Mortgage lending including foreign national loan program
Business investment products (401ks, profit sharing, simple IRAs, pension plans)
Currency exchange and foreign cash certification services
Business merchant services (IBC Merchant Services powered by Basys)
Treasury management tools (repurchase agreements, remote deposit, lockbox service)
Visa debit cards and credit cards (personal and business)
Insurance products (garage, workers compensation, property, business auto, Medicare supplement)
Wire transfers and letters of credit
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full U.S. government backing
Specialized international banking services including foreign national loan program and currency exchange
Multiple checking account options with varying features and interest-bearing options
Comprehensive business services including merchant processing, treasury management, and remote deposit
One-stop financial services offering banking, investment products, mortgages, and insurance
Free financial education resources through Visa's Practical Money Skills program
Publicly traded parent company (NASDAQ: IBOC) with demonstrated dividend payment history
cons: Limited to Oklahoma and Texas markets only—no national branch network
Regional bank with likely fewer digital innovation features compared to national competitors or neobanks
Website navigation is complex with many menu options that may overwhelm casual users
No mention of competitive interest rates, fee structures, or product comparisons in available content
Limited transparency on specific loan terms, credit card APRs, or product pricing details
best_for: Texas and Oklahoma residents seeking FDIC-insured banking with established local branches
Business owners needing integrated banking, merchant services, and insurance products in one institution
International customers or those with cross-border financial needs (foreign nationals, currency exchange requirements)
Customers seeking a full-service bank offering mortgages, investments, and insurance alongside banking
diagnosis: IBC Bank is best for existing residents of Oklahoma and Texas who value regional banking relationships and need integrated financial services including business banking, international services, or insurance products. The main caveat is that this is a regional institution without national presence, so customers outside these states cannot access branch services, and those seeking cutting-edge digital banking may find better alternatives among larger national or neobanks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: FDIC-insured regional bank serving Oklahoma and Texas with personal, business, and international banking services including checking and savings.
fdic_mdi_status: Minority Board and Serving Hispanic Community
